stories.headline: Lazarus owns April: crypto hack toll crests $620M, 95% from one DPRK crew
stories.tldr: April 2026 is the worst month for crypto theft since February 2025, with $620M+ drained across 12 incidents and Lazarus behind the two biggest.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two cross-chain bridges and a single compromised admin key gave one nation-state nearly $580M — the attack surface narrative is no longer code, it's keys and people.
the_details: Kelp DAO bridge drained for $292M on April 19, freezing rsETH collateral across Aave, SparkLend, Fluid and Upshift.
Drift Protocol lost $285M on April 1 via fake-token oracle manipulation through a compromised admin key.
Year-to-date 2026 theft hits ~$771.8M across 47 incidents, per April recap data.

stories.headline: Banks push to slow GENIUS Act rollout as Agora races OCC for stablecoin charter
stories.tldr: Major U.S. banks are asking regulators for extended GENIUS Act comment windows while crypto-native issuer Agora sprints to lock in an OCC charter first.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: First-mover advantage on a federal stablecoin charter could lock in a multi-year moat — banks know it, which is why they're trying to slow the clock.
the_details: Banking groups want extended public comment periods on FDIC and OCC stablecoin rulemakings before full implementation.
Agora is filing for federal trust charter to issue stablecoins under direct OCC oversight.
GENIUS Act enacted late 2025 splits issuers by size — sub-$10B can opt into state regimes, $10B+ move to OCC.

stories.headline: BTC closes April defensive at $76K even as ETFs absorb $1.7B, biggest haul since October
stories.tldr: Bitcoin enters May on the back foot near $76,130 even as spot BTC ETFs absorbed roughly $1.7B in April — the largest monthly inflow since October 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Institutional bid is the only thing keeping BTC propped against 5% 30-year yields and Iran tape — strip ETF flows and the chart looks materially worse.
the_details: BTC opened April 30 at $75,752, lowest since April 13, before bouncing to $76,130 by 7:18 a.m. ET.
April crypto market cap rose ~10% to ~$2.7T; BTC +16% on the month, ETH +14% to ~$2,268.
Macro headwinds — U.S. 30-year yields at 5%, Iran energy risk — capping further upside into May.

stories.headline: BTC spot volume craters into month-end, setting up an asymmetric vol break
stories.tldr: Bitcoin trading volume is falling fast heading into May, a setup that historically resolves with violent directional moves rather than slow drift.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Compressed volume plus crowded long funding equals trapdoor risk on either side — options desks are pricing wider tails into the May FOMC window.
the_details: Spot volumes on top venues at multi-month lows even as ETF flows stay positive.
Whale wallets adding longs while perps funding stays deeply negative — a rare divergence.
Analysts flag $80K overhead resistance and $73K macro support as next break levels.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai shoved to 2027 as UAE security risks force flagship event delay
stories.tldr: Organizers postponed TOKEN2049 Dubai to 2027 citing heightened regional security risks in the UAE tied to the ongoing Iran conflict.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The Gulf has become crypto's de facto deal venue — losing the year's biggest Middle East summit reroutes BD pipelines and capital intros for 12+ months.
the_details: Decision driven by escalating regional tensions and energy-market volatility, per organizers.
TOKEN2049 Singapore remains the flagship Asia event for 2026; Dubai now slated to return 2027.
Knock-on effect on side-event sponsors and token launch calendars eyeing UAE liquidity.
